subject: English Language
q: Write a letter to your friend who is in another school telling him or her the valuable lessons you have learnt during a recent excursion.
explanation:
Government Secondary School

Obio Usiere, Eniong Abatim.

Odukpani L.G.A.

Cross River State.

13th September, 2021.

 

Dear Ekpika,

I am very happy to write this letter to you. How are you and your studies? I hope that you are doing well in all areas of your endeavours. I am quite alright in health and in my studies. My dear friend, Ekpika, I have the pleasure to relate to you the valuable lessons I have learnt on the recent excursion. Our tourism mistress, Mrs. Uionkpan Odey announced to all students in Ss3 that twe will embark on an excursion to Yankari National Park, located in Bauchi State, in the north eastern Nigeria, on 20th May, 2021. On that day, we left Obio Usiere for Bauchi State. The bus carried eighteen students and two teachers including our tourism mistress Mrs. Urionkpan Odey. On the arrival in Bauchi, the state capital, we were directed to Yankari Game reserve in the south-central part of Bauchi state.

The park is the most developed wildlife park in Nigeria with a lot of attractions for visitors. The park is famous for its variety of animals for viewing. We went there in the month of May, because that is the period of the year that the animals converge around the Gaji River, Baboons, Waterbucks, Elephants, Crocodiles, Bushbucks, Duckers, Warthongs, Hippopotamus were some of the animals that we saw at the park

The surrounding environment was very captivating with hills, valleys and plains, beautifully wrapped up, such that one cannot ignore its sceneries that are inspiringly blended. I also learnt that the Yankari national park has another famous attraction- The Wikki warm spring. The spring is particularly prized for its warm water. The spring is available both night and day for excursion and for tourism. It was one of the best experiences I have had in the recent past.

The excursion was an opportunity for me to learn more about the animals in their natural habitat. I saw elephants, crocodiles, hippopotamus and other animals I used to see before on television, face to face.

The excursion was part of the school learning programme. It was a cherished break from the traditional classroom teaching and learning. Indeed travelling is a part of education. The excursion was quite necessary. I took notes of everything we were taught.

We were advised to conduct ourselves in an orderly manner. The manner of the park, Dr. Shehu Bello conducted us to the reception side of the park, and served us a very delicious meal. There, I learnt table manners, especially while eating with important dignitaries and other students.

We were trained on how to swim. Now I can swim without making use of life-jacket. Again, I learnt how to paddle a canoe. Now I can paddle a canoe with a wooden or plastic paddle.

Moreover, the excursion has afforded me the opportunity to know so many states in Nigeria. As a result of the route we took, we passed through Nassarawa, Yobe, Gombe and Adamawa North Eastern part of Nigeria.

Finally, I wish to let you know that the valuable lessons I have learnt from the excursion will live long in my memory and will continue to influence me positively in my future educational pursuit.

Thank you very much for the textbooks you sent to me. Take your studies very seriously. Stay well till l visit you. Bye.

Yours sincerely,

Inyang.

subject: English Language
q: Several articles have been published on the alarming rate of indiscipline among the youth of your country. Write your contribution.
explanation:
ALARMING RATE OF INDISCIPLINE AMONG THE YOUTH OF MY COUNTRY

Indiscipline is the opposite of discipline, Discipline is a situation where every citizen of a country behaves in an orderly manner, obeys the laws of the land and respects the constituted authorities anytime or anywhere. But alas, all these cannot be realized among the youth of my country, Nigeria.

The youth of my country do not respect their parents at home. The youth exhibit immoral behaviours openly, not minding their parents, or other elderly people in the society. They do not have restrain in their conduct. This is so pathetic. The youth then used to respect the elders, possibly helping them in manual and domestic works, but now the story is different. The youth rather insult, abuse and flagrantly disrespect the elderly persons in the society. The youth smoke hallucinogenic drugs like Indian hemp popularly called marijuana, tramadol and all sorts of intoxicants, that make them ruthless and indisciplined.

Another reason responsible for the alarming rate of indiscipline among the youth of my country is the incessant cases of cultism. The youth that joined cults groups believe that they are on top of the world. They feel that they are superior to any other person who is not a member of any cult group, claiming that since they have access to guns and other dangerous weapons, they can maim, injure or even kill anybody that might want to restrain them on his way.

Another factor responsible for the alarming rate of indiscipline among the youth of my county is the inducement by our politicians. The politicians used the youth to cause mayhem to political opponents during elections, by providing them with guns and other dangerous weapons. After the election, the politicians may not be able to retrieve all the weapons from these youth. The youth will in turn use these weapons for armed robbery, kidnapping and on many occasions, the youth use the weapons to attack law enforcement agents, like the police or the army. Cases abound where such youth attacked the police in their stations, killed the officers and collect AK 47 from them. In most cases the culprits are caught and detained for further investigations, only to be released by the police because some of the leading politicians have gone to the station and paid a large sum of money to bail them out of detention, One may ask, who are those supporting the indiscipline of the youth is attributed to the decay in the societal my country? The answer is the politicians.

Moreover, the indiscipline of the youth in the country can be attributed to the decay in societal norms. The traditional values like respect, honesty, humility, integrity and dignity of labour aré no longer held in high esteem. Now the youth of my country do not want to submit themselves to the traditional and cultural heritage of their fatherland, They tend to imbibe foreign culture which has affected their psychological, social and moral orientation.

Another reason for the alarming increase in the rate of indiscipline among the youth of my country is that the youth want success without hard work. They placed much premium on money without minding who is hurt or not, provided they get the money. The parents and the society do not help matters. The parents push their children into drug abuse, prostitution, smuggling. thuggery. armed robbery and other crimes just to make money. Our philosophy of hard work and honesty are thrown into the wind.

The social media is another factor that has contributed to the alarming rate of indiscipline among the youth of my country. In our schools you see students coming to school with cell phones. Some have downloaded pornographic pictures and videos and foreign materials that do not build their moral behaviours. Even they walk along the streets with ear-piece fixed in their ears. This has caused them to be partially insensitive to their immediate environment. Some even get involved in avoidable accidents as a result of careless behaviours.

The churches and the mosques do not help our youth. The youth go to church and mosques dressed like prostitutes or hooligans. They dress half-naked to the church or mosque. They dress half-naked to the church or mosque. They do not reference God in his sanctuary. Some will be busy pressing their phones while prayers and sermons are going on.. some even discuss in the church while worship is going on. This is the greatest act of indiscipline.

What can we do to reverse this ugly trend of indiscipline among the youth of my country? The answers to this question are not far-fetched, The parents have an important role to play in this regard. They must inculcate moral discipline in their children. They must show examples to their children. They must show examples in behaviour and in character. They must encourage the youth by promoting good social values that will make them better citizens of this country.

Our society should also promote values that will make every member of Our society to be disciplined. Criminal attitudes and deviant behaviours should be jettisoned. The youth should not be used as political thugs, because after their violent and criminal actions during elections, they will still remain in the society indisciplined. Again, political godfatherism should be discouraged. Any recalcitrant behaviour should be punished severly. Our education planners should include moral discipline in the school curriculum, to educate the students on sound moral attitudes in the society. Ill-gotten wealth should be investigated and the culprits be brought to book.

If all the points suggested above are strictly considered, then alarming rate of indiscipline in my country, Nigeria.

Richard Oyama.

subject: English Language
q: Cases of malaria have been on the increase in recent times in your country. Write a letter to the Minister of Health, discussing your observation and suggesting at least three ways of curbing the disease.
explanation:
Community Secondary School,

Old Netim, Akamkpa L.G.A

Cross River State, Nigeria.

13th of September, 2021.

 

Honourable Minister of Health,

Federal Secretariat complex,

Phase ll, Shehu Shagari way.

Central Business District

Abuja, F.C.T. Nigeria.

Sir,

CASES OF MALARIA INCREASE IN RECENT TIMES IN MY COUNTRY, NIGERIA.

I wish to bring to your notice cases of malaria íncrease in my country, Nigeria. Every day in the last six months had witnessed a rapid increase of malaria in all the thirty-six states in my country, Nigeria. According to medical reports, about six hundred people have died in the last six months as a result of malaria.

Malaria is a parasitic disease caused by plasmodium, carried by female anopheles mosquito bites. Some of the symptoms include high fever, associated with shivering, sweating and sometimes severe dace. Malaria is deadly if proper medication is not administered by trained medical personnel, For example, in Kogi, Taraba and Akwa lbom states, the people resorted to self-medication and traditional methods for the treatment of malaria, The resultant effects were massive death of the citizens of those states in Nigeria.

Malaria is a parasitic disease. it can be transmitted from infected person to uninfected person through a vector- an animal which transmits a disease-causing organism called pathogen, from the person suffering the disease to another person. In the case of malaria, the female mosquito is the carrier of the disease.

The government of the Federal Republic of Nigeria through the Federal Ministry of Health should rise-up to the challenge posed by the deadly disease called malaria, in the country. No problem is insurmountable if all hands are on deck. The Government of the Federal Republic of Nigeria in conjunction with the federal ministry of health should do the following in order to curb or even eradicate malaria from the country. The entire population of this country must be educated on how to maintain good hygiene. Drainage of swamps that serve as the breeding ground for mosquitoes and clearing of bushes around our houses should be done to reduce the hiding places for adult mosquitoes. Broken pots and empty cans should be buried to eliminate possible breeding places for mosquitoes. Stagnant water should be drained or oiled sprayed on the stagnant water to reduce the Surface tension of water and prevent larval stages of mosquitoes from breathing, hence they will die.

The Government should provide drugs like chloroquine to cure malaria fever. The government should also provide every household with an insecticide mosquito treated net and enforce all the citizens to use the net, to prevent mosquito bites. Insect repellent should be used on the body to repel or prevent mosquito bites. Also the use of biological control like the use of predators in the mosquito habitat to eliminate or eat up the mosquito larvae.

The Ministry of Health in conjunction with the Nigerian Association should advise the government on how to improve the health status of the citizens of Nigeria. They should also alert the country when and where there is an outbreak of diseases like malaria and carry out research into ways of preventing and controlling diseases.

Finally, the Ministry of Health should monitor the recruitment of well-trained doctors in the hospitals and assist in the training and re-training of medical and para-medical staff needed in health care delivery. if all the suggestions given above are implemented malaria will be curbed if not totally eradicated in my country, Nigeria.

Yours Faithfully,

Ekpo Ita Bassey.

subject: English Language
q: You have just been awarded a scholarship by a local company. Write a speech you would deliver at the presentation ceremony stating the benefits of the scholarship.
explanation:
The honourable chairman of the occasion, managing director, indomitable mining company of Nigeria, our respected principal, members of the parent-teachers association, fellow students, ladies and gentlemen.

It is with great delights and immense pleasure that I stand before this great gathering to deliver a speech on the occasion of the presentation ceremony and award of scholarship to me. I consider myself very lucky to be asked as a beneficiary of the scholarship award by the indomitable mining company of Nigeria, to address a meeting of this kind, today. Before I begin my speech I must, first of all, congratulate the indomitable mining of Nigeria, whose headquarters is based n Akure, the Ondo state capital.

The indomitable mining company has raised a substantial among of money for my scholarship award. Though the processes for the selection of the best candidate for the av ard of the scholarship was rigorous as members of the committee for the selection were very strict, fair and unbias in their selection, I finally emerged as the overall best candidate for the award of the scholarship. Undoubtedly I was as happy as a king when my name was mentioned as the best for the award of the scholarship. The managing director congratulated me and expressed the hope that I would justify the confidence that the company has reposed on me.

The benefits of the scholar to me state as follows: that, the summed total of the school fees for six years shall be borned by the conmpany. This, therefore, means that I shall not pay school fees throughout my stay in the secondary school. This is a very great relief to my parents, who before now will suffer to pay my school fees.

The scholarship also covers my feeding and hostel accommodation. before this time, I was a day student, and some days I do not have money to pay my fare to and from school. Now I am a boarder; living in the hostel, being fed three times a day with sumptuous meals and snacks. I can now say bye-bye to malnourishment. I am now in the pink; I am very healthy.

Above all, I have peace of mind. Gone are the days that I will be fretting about things that are not there. Now, honestly speaking, I have peace of mind and I can now concentrate on my studies.

May I use this opportunity to congratulate all who have made his award ceremony a success. I must particularly thank the managing director of indomitable mining company, in the person of Prof. Adams John Odey for his magnanimity. May God bless you, sir. Again, I will not fail to thank the principal of Community Secondary School, Akpap Okoyong, Mr. Inyang Bassey for his professional skills in piloting the affairs of this great institution. may God bless you abundantly. Worthy also to be congratulated are the vice principal, Mr. Paul Effiong Asuquo, staff and all the students of Community Secondary School Akpap Okoyong for their moral support during the selection process which saw me emerging as the overall best for the award of this scholarship. Thank you all.

I must not fail to thank my parents, Mr. and Mrs. Godwin Oden, for their love and care. I would like to be born to them again and again. You are good parents to me indeed.

Finaly, I have promised to justify the confidence reposed in me. I shall live up to the expectations required of me. Thank you all as you have honoured the invitation to grace this occasion despite the Short notice. May God bless you all and make your journey back to your different home safe once again, thank you all.

Blossom Godwin Oden.

subject: English Language
q: Write a story that ends with the statement: Better late than never.
explanation:
One of our students Miss Janet Evong, was bereaved of her father. The students, Union Government agreed that all the students in the school should attend that and witness the burial rites. For this reason, all the students were asked to pay ten thousand naira each to facilitate them to pay for the transport fare from Oshodi, Lagos to Maitama in Abuja.

Every student was to assemble at Oshodi central motor park the following day, being Saturday, 18th September 2021, for onward movement to Maitama, Abuja. The D-day came about fiity-seven students were ready for the onward movement from Oshodi central motor park to Maitama Abuja. It was a day of mourning for some of the students and a day of excitement for some other students, as they had never travelled long distances in their lives before now.

The bus was a long luxurious bus that had the capacity of carrying sixty passengers at a time.All the fifty-seven students boarded the bus. The students union president, Mr. Francis Ayum, prayed to God for the safe journey of the students from Oshodi to Maitama.

The driver started the engine, and the journey commences. The first impression of misgiving that the students had about the competence of the driver was when he made an attempt to round a bend. His right tyre served to the wrong side of the untared road. There would have been a fatal accident, but it was averted as the students shouted at the driver.

Again, when they got to cross-roads, the traffic light shone red, the driver managed to stop. the green light did not show when the driver moved his car. The driver was reckless, not observing the road signs or traffic regulations. The students at this time developed greater fear in their minds about the proficiency of the driver. They complained and started scolding the driver over his reckless driving.

The last straw that broke the camel's back was when he wrongly overtook another car, not judging the distance between his bus and another incoming picnic. It was by divine intervention, the two cars would have collided. At this juncture, the students could not bear it any longer as Joy Okafor, one of the female students shouted that she was not ready to die prematurely. Other students joined her, they started to cry aloud against the driver's reckless driving.

The driver, in his reaction, told the students that, he wants to reach Abuja before the burial ceremonies begin. lIt was there and then that the students came to the realization of what prompted the driver to be impatient, careless and reckless in his driving. The students union president, Mr Francis Ayum cautioned the driver that it is good for them to arrive safely and that it is better late than never.

q:
The word that receives the emphatic stress is in capital letters. From the questions lettered A to D, choose the one to which the given sentence relates.

All the EMPLOYEES rejected him.
options: Did all the employees reject him
Did some of the employees reject him?
Did all the employees accept him?
Did all the employers reject him?
explanation:
Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word or phrase in a sentence or utterance. For questions on emphatic stress, the rule is to select the option that has a word that contradicts the emphasised word/ word in capital letters. In this case, the answer is option D. because it has "employers" instead of "employees". This makes sense because the sentence is the appropriate response to the question as it clarifies a misapprehension in the question:

Did all the employers reject him?

No. All the employees rejected him.

q:
In each of the following sentences, there is one underlined word and one gap. From the list of words lettered A-D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will, at the same time correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

Obi's family is known for their audacity, so one is surprised at his ______
explanation: The word "audacity" implies boldness or daring behaviour. To maintain the contrast in the sentence, we are looking for a word that represents the opposite of audacity, the word that contrasts with audacity is: A. cowardice So, the complete sentence would be: "Obi's family is known for their audacity, so one is surprised at his cowardice."

subject: English Language
q:
Fron the options lettered A-D, choose the word(s) that is opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined and that will, at the same time, most appropriately fill the gap in the sentence. 

Bayo is careless in his ways while Tayo is ________
explanation: Let’s look at the contrast implied by "while"—this conjunction signals opposition or contrast. We need a word that contrasts with "careless." "Meticulous" means very careful and precise, which is the direct opposite of careless.

subject: English Language
q:
From the words lettered A. to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word or group of words that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that would appropriately fill the gap in the following sentence:

In this country, the law against armed robbery is stringent whereas that against  smoking in public places is.........
explanation:
• Stringent: Refers to something that is strict, precise, or severe.

  • Lax: Refers to something that is not strict, loose, or lenient.

Given that the law against armed robbery is described as stringent, the appropriate opposite to complete the sentence would be "lax," indicating that the law against smoking in public places is not as strict or severe.

subject: English Language
q: The purpose of education is not to..... but to facilitate progress
explanation:
The word "impede" means to hinder, obstruct, or slow down progress. In the context of the sentence, the statement contrasts the purpose of education with the idea of hindrance or restraint. The sentence conveys that education is not meant to prevent or impede progress but, instead, to facilitate or promote it.

Option B, "impede," accurately captures the idea of creating obstacles or barriers, making it the most suitable choice for the intended meaning in the sentence.

subject: English Language
q: These days, many people concentrate on temporal things to the detriment of the _______
explanation:
The sentence expresses the idea that many people are giving undue attention to temporary or worldly matters, and this focus is negatively affecting another aspect. The missing word should represent the opposite or a contrasting aspect to "temporal" or worldly.

  • Temporal refers to things that are related to time, temporary, or material.

  • Spiritual represents matters related to the spirit, soul, or the non-material aspects of life, often associated with beliefs, values, and a sense of purpose.

Therefore, the most suitable word to complete the sentence is "spiritual," as it contrasts with "temporal" and reflects the idea that people are neglecting spiritual aspects in favour of temporary or material concerns.

subject: English Language
q: While your parents are ........ ., mine are liberal in their views.
explanation:
"Conservative" refers to an inclination to preserve existing conditions and institutions, or to limit change. It can also describe a cautious or moderate approach, especially in political or social matters. In this sentence, it suggests that one set of parents is more traditional or cautious in their views.

On the other hand, the term "liberal" indicates an openness to new ideas, progress, and social reform. The sentence implies that the speaker's parents hold more progressive or open-minded views compared to the conservative views of the other parents.

subject: English Language
q:
FIll in the gap with the appropriate option in the sentence below:

The judge expected direct answers but the accused was ....
explanation: The most appropriate option to complete the sentence is D. evasive. The sentence suggests that the accused did not provide direct answers, and the term "evasive" fits means to avoid giving direct responses or being intentionally vague in your answers.

subject: English Language
q:
Select the option that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence. 

 Kofi is generous, whereas his wife is
explanation: The word "whereas" in the sentence indicates a contrast between Kofi's generosity and his wife's trait. The opposite of "generous" is "stingy," which means unwilling to share or give.

subject: English Language
q: His manners are uncouth, while his wife's are ___________.
explanation: Uncouth means lacking good manners, refinement or grace. The opposite of this would be someone polished and well-mannered. Refined means elegant, cultured and well-mannered. Therefore, C. refined is the correct answer.

subject: English Language
q: The peak of Mount Everest is about six miles from its _______
explanation: The "base" of a mountain refers to the lowest part where it begins to rise. Since the peak of Mount Everest is about six miles above its starting point, "base" is the most appropriate word to complete the sentence

subject: English Language
q: The recent floods in some countries have _______ many lives
explanation: The word "claimed" is often used in the context of disasters, accidents, or tragic events to indicate that lives were lost. When we say that lives were "claimed," it implies that they were taken or lost as a result of the floods.

subject: English Language
q: He does not smoke so the gift of an ashtray will not be quite _________

subject: English Language
q: The reviewer did an excellent job at the book ______

subject: English Language
q: The governor filed an _______ against the ruling of the tribunal.
explanation: An "appeal" is a formal request to a higher court or authority for a review and possible reversal of a decision made by a lower court or tribunal. In the context of the sentence, the governor is dissatisfied with the ruling of the tribunal and is taking legal action by filing an appeal. An appeal is a common legal term used when someone challenges a decision and seeks a reconsideration from a higher judicial body. The other options, such as "injunction," "objection," and "application," do not specifically convey the idea of seeking a review of a tribunal ruling.

subject: English Language
q: At the terminus, all the passengers on the train will have to ________
explanation: The word “disembark” means to get off a train, ship, or aircraft at the end of a journey, while "alight” means to get off a bus or other road vehicle.

q: The economy of the country has remained _______ despite the crisis
explanation:
The best answer is A. strong because it best describes an economy that has weathered a crisis and remained in a positive, resilient state. The other options do not capture this meaning as effectively.

"Realistic" suggests the economy is grounded in facts, but does not necessarily indicate strength or resilience.
"Tough" implies the economy has faced difficulties, but does not convey that it has remained stable.
"Firm" suggests the economy has maintained a steady, unwavering state, which is similar to "strong" but not as concise and powerful a descriptor.

Therefore, the correct answer is A. strong.

q: In the following passage, the numbered gap 71 indicates a missing word. Four options are given in columns lettered A, to D. Choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the gap in the passage.
explanation:
In the context of the passage, the sentence talks about medical experts emphasizing a particular approach within the last quarter of the twentieth century. The word that aligns well with this emphasis and fits the context is "preventive." Preventive measures are actions taken to prevent or avoid something, and in the context of health, it refers to measures taken to prevent diseases or health issues.

Therefore, the complete sentence would be: "Within the last quarter of the twentieth century, medical experts have been laying emphasis on preventive rather than defensive measures." This means that the emphasis is on measures that prevent health issues rather than defensive measures taken after the issues have already occurred.

explanation:
In the context of the passage, the sentence talks about an individual experiencing a disease. The most appropriate word to fill the gap in this context is "contracts." When an individual contracts a disease, it means that they have acquired or become infected with the disease. Option B, "contracts," fits well with the meaning conveyed in the sentence.

The other options:

  • A. acquires: While this is a possible synonym, "contracts" is more commonly used in the context of diseases.
  • C. contacts: The correct verb in this context is "contracts" rather than "contacts."
  • D. develops: While individuals can develop diseases, the sentence is more focused on the acquisition or contraction of a disease.

Therefore, option B, "contracts," is the most suitable choice.

subject: English Language
q: The principal punished the students for being DISHONEST.
options: Did the teacher punish the students for being dishonest?
Did the principal praise the students for being dishonest?
Did the principal punish the students for being sincere?
Did the principal punish the teachers for being dishonest?
explanation:
Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in an utterance. This is done for emphasis, clarification or correction. In this case, the response is designed to correct a misapprehension of the questioner. The questioner wrongly assumed that the students were being punished by the principal for being "sincere". The responder corrects this misapprehension by emphasising "dishonest". 

Did the principal punish the students for being sincere?

No. the principal punished the students for being DISHONEST.

subject: English Language
q: Write a story illustrates the saying: Uneasy lies the head that wears a crown.
explanation:
My father was the village head of Amanza Kingdom in Jigawa State. He ascended the throne at the age of 27 years old. The throne is ascended by hereditary. It is always the senior male child of the village head that will emerge as the new village head of the Amanza people. When my father was alive, we lack nothing in our house, because all the tributes, fine, dues, booties from war, proceeds from the sales of farmlands and other money generating ventures in the community were handed over to my father. We fed fat from all these, with a merry heart.
When my father died, I was crowned the new village head of the Amanza Kingdom. I was so happy that I was going to control all that resources in the kingdom as my father did, when he was alive. Little did I know what my father passed through as the village head of the Kingdom. Numerous
land cases, settlement of disputes of all sorts, series of meetings: both day and night, offering of sacrifices before planting seasons, sacrifices before harvesting of yam and new yam festivals, were too cumbersome for me. I hardly sleep with my two eyes closed or have time for my family. Many a
time, my wife hardly sees me. My wife eats alone, stays in the house without my company. My children also scarcely see me. One day my last born saw me in the day time eating in the parlour, she ran to the mother and told her mother "One man is eating our food in the parlour". My wife answered
her "he is your father". The girl asked the mother why she had never seen me before at home. The mother told her that, she always sleeps when l leave early in the morning and come back late at night when she is fast asleep
It is a tough time for me. I used to be very healthy but now, I am sickly I wake up early in the morning and stay throughout the day without resting. Sometimes, I will stay with my cabinet members throughout the night without sleep, deliberating on matters affecting the communities. Despite all the
wealth I have as the village head of Amanza Kingdom, l am healthy enough to enjoy my wealth . I have now become a problem for myself and my family. I as a person with great power, a king in my kingdom, I am constantly apprehensive. I cannot find a moment's peace and repose. As a king who has great responsibilities, i am constantly worried and therefore, i don't sleep soundly.
I have now come to see why my father was always preoccupied with the affairs of the Amanza  Kingdom. I have also experienced it. Therefore, the saying: uneasy lies the head that wears a crown it true, the king has no time for himself.

subject: English Language
q: A new principal has just been posted to your school. As the senior prefect, write a welcome address on behalf of the students, pointing out three areas of need in the school.
explanation:
A WELCOME ADDRESS PRESENTED TO MR. ADETUSA ADEBISI, THE NEW. PRINCIPAL POSTED TO USMAN DAN FODIO MEMORIAL GRAMMAR SCHOOL, BAUCHI, BAUCHI STATE, NIGERIA.

BY MUSA KAREEM, THE SENIOR PREFECT OF THE SCHOOL, AT THE SCHOOL HALL ON 9TH JUNE, 2022.

Our honoured Principal, Mr. Adetusa Adebisi,
The Vice Principal-Academic and Administration,
Respected Members of the staff,
The Parents-Teachers Association,
Fellow students,
reolboHrnoel
Ladies and Gentlemen,
l am very happy and delighted to be given the privilege on a unique occasion like this to present
an address of welcome in honour of our new principal, Mr. Adetusa Adebisi, just posted to this school, to continue his good works as administrator of schools in Nigeria. Today marks a new era in the continuity of the administration of this ancient institution. We are greatly honoured to have in our midst a distinguished teacher and school administrator, Mr. Adetusa Adebisi, who has arrived to take up the principalship of this great college.
If l am not mistaken, Sir, you are 25th Principal posted to this school, since the inception of this great citadel of learning in 1922, This shows that the school is the oldest among the schools in the state. I regard your acceptance to the headship of this school as indicative of the great interest you
have in the welfare of our school. It is a noble gesture of which we are really proud of you. Mr. Adetusa Adebisi, my fellow students and l consider your posting to this college a wonderful privilege. We have therefore, organised this special meeting to express our delight and to give you a warm reception. We heartily welcome you to this great institution of learning.
We have followed your career as a school principal with great interest. Your presence in any school has always brought about academic excellence and discipline. We are fully convinced that you have the zeal and talent to nurture the young to greatness. Your posting to our school is, indeed, the beginning of great expectations. We believe that you will find the necessary courage and strength to teach and direct us on to new paths. We are grateful to the State Ministry of Education for sending you to our school.
One of the most important areas of need is poor staffing and unqualified teachers in the school. We wish that you laise with the State Ministry of Education in conjunction with the State Secondary Education Board to recruit and post enough and qualified teaching staff in the relevant subject areas to the school. The areas of need are English Language, Mathematics, Chemistry, Physics, Biology, Literature in English, Civic Education, Economics, Accounting and others. This will help to improve the performance of the students in both internal and external examinations. For the past years the school had experienced mass failures in West African Senior School Certificate Examinations Council (NECO). We are confident that with you as the principal of our school, we shall overcome the need of the school in this direction.                                                                                                                                                     Another important area of need in the school is lack of standard laboratories. Our laboratories are poorly equipped. The teaching of science subjects have become nothing but a shame. The Chemistry laboratories should be separated from the Physics and Biology laboratories. The three
core science subjects should have separate science laboratories for effective teachings and leaning of science in the school. In order to improve the standard of education in the great school, therefore the teachers should leave no stone unturned in order to ensure that the students are properly taught.
If the students are well taught, they will pass their examinations well and gain admission into universities without undue delay.
Another area of need that demands your serious attention is unauthorised levies and dues. There are various recognised levies that the Government has authorised parents to pay, but the past principals of this school had subjected the parents to pay other numerous unauthorised levies and dues for their wards in the school. All these payments are made by the same parents who pay tuition fees, boarding fees as well as providing money for books, clothes, pocket money and other things. Some students dropped out of school as a result of their parents inability to pay all the authorised and unauthorised levies and dues for their wards. Collection of unauthorised levies and dues should be stopped in this school. This will be the only way to reduce the financial burden on parents and make the lives of the students worth living.
Mr. Principal Sir, we believe that, God has sent you as a messiah to redeem this great institution from imminent collapse. We promise to give you maximum co-operation in your endeavour to restore the dignity of this institution. We have no doubt that you will attract highly proficient staff and better government support for the school during your stay. Once again we wish you a hearty welcome and
many more years of successful school administration.
Thanks.
                                                                                                                                             Musa Kareem
                                                                                                                                             Senior Prefect
                                                                                                                                             Usman Dan Fodio Memorial Grammar School, Bauchi.

explanation:
Comprehensive High School
                                                                                                                                                         Isong Inyang Abatim

                                                                                                                                                         9th June, 2022.
The Principal,
Comprehensive High School,
Isong Inyang Abatim.
P.O.Box 9341.
Calabar
Cross River State.

Dear Sir,
                                           Congratulatory message on the occasion of 60th Anniversary Celebration.
I wish to congratulate our able erudite and competent principal, Mr. Ituen Okokon on the occasion of the 60th anniversary celebration of our great institution, Comprehensive High School Isong Inyang Abatim, Calabar, Cross River State. It is a thing of great joy that this great citadel of learning has whethered the storm for these number of years.
Without any fear of contradiction, this school is the oldest school in this part of Nigeria, after Hope Waddel Training Institution. Comprehensive High School, Isong Inyang Abatum has produced eminents and world class scholars, who have excelled in different fields of human Among these scholars produced by our great institution are Senator Michael Uche, Dr Moses Ndifon, Rev. father Titus Jeremiah just to mention a few.
For this occasion, Sir, I use this medium to thank you for your landmark achievements in this great institution. Your wealth of experience and your high academic stand has made you a competent school administrator of this great institution. No one else is competent enough to pilot and manage
the affairs of this school but you. This great courage has been repositioned to gain back her lost glory all to your own credit.
Dear Sir, l as the former Senior Prefect of this great institution use this opportunity of the occasion of both anniversary celebration suggest that, these three areas in the school need improvement.
Firstly, the area of sanitation in the school needs to be improved upon. The school need a standard toilet, to meet the demands of the teaming population growth in the school. The toilet should be built with modern facilities. You can bear me witness that the students defecations have litered the whole school because of lack of good toilet facilities. The students should be cautioned on how to keep themselves and the school compound clean at all times. Dumping of refuse indiscriminately in the school should be addressed with all amount of seriousness by the school authority.
Secondly, the school needs to improve in the area of sporting activities. For some years now the school has not organised inter-house sport competitions. This has affected the school in the area of sporting activities. For instance, during the last Governor's cup football competition, the school
crashed out at the group stage in that competition.
This was very shameful indeed. A standard football field should be built in the school. Other sporting events should be organised in the school, like volleyball, lawn tennis and others. Field events facilities like. Javelin, discuss and others should be made available in the school. Athletic events should also be improved upon. The loss glory of the school in sporting activities should be restored in a no distant time.
Thirdly there should be improvement in the area of students staff relationship. A Careful obsevation shows that there is no cordial relationship between the students and the staff of this school. The teachers are harsh to the students. The teachers do not create rooms for students to approach them for counselling. The students rather received wrong advice from the wrong people. This has affected the conducts and behaviours of the students. Some teachers do not keep secrets revealed to them by the students. The teachers should be cautioned on how to handle such secrets from the students. The female staff should be made to counsel the female students and in the same vein, the male teachers should be made to counsel the male, except in the area of specialty, but let one other person be present while the counselling is going on.
I therefore thank you personally for piloting the affairs of this school descreetly and judiciously. If all the areas mentioned are give priority attention, I assure you that you will have a successful tenure in your stay in this school.
Thank you very much.

                                                                                                                                                 Yours faithfully,

                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                  Emmanuel Akposi

subject: English Language
q: The National Scholarship Secretariat is organising an essay competition on the topic: The menace of fake drugs in the society. Write your entry.
explanation:
The menace of fake drugs in the society

A reliable good-quality drug supply is essential for good health. But often it is missing in society where men and women are dubious. Falsified and substandard medicines, poisoning, lead to untreated disease, early death and treatment failure. Fake drug could be seen as pharmaceutical fraud, a situation where there are substandard drugs in circulation,  contaminated products used as drugs, a deliberate fraud by a medical dealer. Fake drug may contain toxic doses of dangerous ingredients and substances that make poor-quality medicines that can never be effective in the treatment of chronic and infectious diseases, causing progression, drug resistance and finally death. Fake drugs is a very serious menace that has threatened the health of the populations in the society today and in the future.
The crux of the matter and the more insidious problem is medicine that does not work ineffective medicines  often contains benign ingredients. such as chalk pollen or flour, instead of medical chemicals. More dangerously some fake drugs contain substances intended to mask the illness and feign treatment. Patients taking such drugs die with the diseases not effectively treated with original drugs. Drugs are intended to cure patients, or at least to relieve symptoms or show the progression of a disease.
Fake drugs affect people in the society. The users of fake drugs are exposed to serious health complication that can affect vital body organs and their functions. Such cases damages the brain, dame other body organs such as liver, heart, lungs and kidney. It can also lead to drug related
diseases, such as irregular heart-beat, high blood pressure and others.
Fake drugs can also lead to death in some cases, because if drugs are administered with the intention of curing diseases and the drugs are without the vital ingredients or substances.It will not cure the disease, and the patient may eventually die as a result of the fake drugs. It has also been observed by clinical psychologists that fake drugs has caused some individuals to be incapacitated in all aspects of their lives some men are impotent and some women are barren for life.
Fake drugs can also affect the unborn babies. Some babies are born dead, some are born imbeciled, some loss sense of good judgment, some have deviant behaviours, some are very lazy and weak to do any task. Some times the foetuses are damaged. The resultant effects could be stillborn or ectopic pregnancy, all because of fake drugs.
Must we continue to live with the menace of fake drugs in our society? must we continue to mourn as our love ones pass away to the great beyond? The answer is no! We must not continue in this way. Then what must we do to cure or totally eradicate the menace of fake drugs in our society?                      The government in conjunction with the relevant drugs law enforcement agencies must ensure that offenders in fake drugs and counterfeit drugs are made to face the full weight of the law and severely punished. Any person who has the equipment or materials used in the production of fake drugs should be jailed without an option of fine.
Laws should be promulgated prohibiting the use of drugs without the doctors prescription. No medical personnel should be allowed to practise without authenticated licence.
The government should regulate, and control the importation, exportation, manufacturing, distributing, sale, advertising medical equipment and chemicals.
The agencies should periodically conduct inspections of all premises used for the production of food, drugs, cosmetic and other related items and issue certificate of usage on those that are original and publicly destroy the ones that are fake or substandard. They must also ensure that every product has manufacturing and expiration dates boldly written on the pack. Imported drugs must be ascertained and certified before clearance for use from the agencies are issued.                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The religious leaders  should use the Holy Books like the Bible, Quran etc. to teach the dangers of fake drugs in the society. Every individual in the family, villages, Local Government Areas, State and the nation at large should be educated on the dangers of fake and counterfeit drugs There should be material and cash rewards to those who give useful information leading to the arrest of fake drugs barons.
If we are sincere and serious with our health  we must put into practice, all the above mentioned ways of eradicating the menace of fake drugs in the society, we shall live long and enjoy our lives on earth. Thanks.

                                                                                                                                                                                               Njoku Okoro

subject: English Language
q: Write a letter to your friend in another school telling him or her about your future career and how it will be beneficial to your country.
explanation:
St. Patrick College
                                                                                                                                                                           PO. Box 63
                                                                                                                                                                           Abeokuta
                                                                                                                                                                           Ogun State.
                                                                                                                                                                           9th June, 2022.
DearAdewale,
I am very happy to write this letter to you in a most respectful manner, fully conscious of the fact that you are studying well. How is your health and your studies? I hope that everything is running smoothly in your school. Dear friend, it is a thing of joy for me to write this letter to you after one year of silence between two of us. I could recalled that the last time I received your letter was on the 6th of April, 2021. This is not normal considering how closed we are to each other.
Dear Ade, I am writing this letter to intimate you of my future career and how it will be a beneficial to my country, Nigeria. I earnestly want to red medicine in the university. I want to be a medical doctor. This is a profession that I like, cherish and have passion for. I always envy medical practitioners because they are well-respected in the society. Though the profession is highly competitive, I have aptitude for science and desire to came for others.
As a medical doctor, i shall be beneficial to my country, Nigeria, by helping others in an incredibly significant way. When someone is sick or injured, his or her normal way of life is disrupted, as a doctor I have the incredible opportunity to restore these people's lives to normalcy and even save some from
death. Therefore, l being a medical doctor will be extremely rewarding to my country, Nigeria. As a medical doctor, I will have the ability to build meaningful relationships with my patients. By seeing my patients over and over again, I shall be able to build meaningful friendly relationships with them.
Again, if l am a medical doctor in the nearest future, I shall learn everything there is to know about the human body. I shall have the opportunity to study and further examine human body with the innovative technology. From the pumping of the heart to drugs that take away pain. to machines that allows me examine human anatomy. Using modern fascinating.
Moreover, I want to be a medical doctor so that I can speak to my patients on the need for exercise and physical activities, To inform them about the benefits of exercise for good health. Also to motivate my patients to adopt a better healthy lifestyle and better exercise habits.
Furthermore, I want to be beneficial to my country as a medical doctor by providing information about suitable health orientation.
Medicine is a notable profession or career. The satisfaction gained from treating a human being is one of the key reasons I have chosen this career. Relieving a patient's pain or bringing a patient back from the verge of death provides enormous gratification that no other profession can equal.
In addition, why I want to study medicine is that, I can play an important role in altering societal norms and taboos. For example, I shall educate all members of my country about the dangers of smoking or speak out against certain practices such as open defecation. From here my patients will need my advice and live healthy life.
In the same vein, having the opportunity to heal, relieve pain and cure humanity, the credit for a longer lifespan and higher quality of life belongs completely to medical advancement and research. I shall do research as a medical doctor and give the government information on how to prevent outbreak of epidemics and illnesses that would have wiped away people in my country. As a surgeon, shall diagnose complex conditions, perform intricate operation and develop cutting-edge surgical techniques, which is intellectually challenging work for the people of my country. I shall be beneficial in
treating debilitating mental health conditions,
 As part of me helping others in my country recover from illness and lead a healthier lifestyle that can promote quality of life and longevity, I shall build my private hospital and treat patients free of charge. I shall continually seek new methods and medications to treat and improve the overall functioning of the body of the sick in my country.
With these and many others that I shall do, I shall be immense benefit to my country Nigeria, If I succeed to become a medical doctor.
Thanks and have a nice time till we meet. Greet everybody over there for me.
                                                                                                                                                                 Yours affectionately,
                                                                                                                                                                  Ajanaku

explanation: The word 'posh' refers to something stylish, elegant, or luxurious. The opposite of 'posh' in this context would be something simple or unpretentious, hence the word 'modest' is the correct answer.

explanation: The word 'trivial' refers to something of little value or importance. The opposite of 'trivial' would be something of great importance or seriousness, hence the word 'serious' is the correct answer.

explanation: The word 'dogged' refers to something persistent or tenacious. The opposite of 'dogged' would be something uncertain or fluctuating, hence the word 'wavering' is the correct answer.

subject: English Language

explanation: The correct word to complete the sentence is 'protest'. The aggrieved customers staged a fierce protest on the street. This is because the word 'protest' is commonly used to describe a public expression of objection or disapproval, often in opposition to something the protestors perceive as unjust.

This is because a 'hearing' is a legal term used to describe a session in which a case is brought before a judge to provide information and evidence to allow the judge to make a decision.

This is because 'physique' refers to the physical structure or build of a person, especially as it relates to the muscular structure. Athletes are often recognized by their well-built physique.

In this context, it means that the circumstances surrounding the death of the journalist are still unclear or hidden.

subject: English Language

This is because 'homicide' is a legal term for any killing of a human being by another human being. In this context, it refers to the crime the defendant was charged with for causing his neighbour's death.

subject: English Language
explanation: The term 'bout' is used to refer to a short period of intense activity of a specified kind, especially a boxing or wrestling match. Therefore, in the context of the passage, 'bout' is the most suitable word to fill the gap.

subject: English Language
explanation: In the context of boxing, the term 'ring' is used to refer to the small square area in which the boxing match takes place. Therefore, 'ring' is the most suitable word to fill the gap.

subject: English Language
explanation: In professional boxing, the announcer calls the boxers into the ring and introduces them to the audience, providing details such as their name, weight class, and other relevant information.

subject: English Language
explanation: In boxing, 'boxing gloves' are worn by boxers to protect their hands and their opponent's body during the match. Therefore, 'boxing gloves' is the most suitable word to fill the gap.

subject: English Language
explanation: In the context of a boxing match, the term 'opponent' is used to refer to the person who is competing against another in a contest. Therefore, 'opponent' is the most suitable word to fill the gap.

subject: English Language
explanation: In the context of boxing, the term 'hook' is used to describe a type of punch. A hook involves a punch that is thrown in a curved manner. Other options like 'knock', 'kick', and 'smack' are not specific boxing terms and hence are not suitable in this context

subject: English Language
explanation: In boxing, punches are thrown with 'fists'. The term 'fists' is more appropriate in this context as it refers to the hand when the fingers are bent in towards the palm and held there tightly, typically in order to strike a blow or grasp something. The other options 'hands', 'elbow', and 'palms' are not used to throw punches in boxing.

subject: English Language
explanation: 'Parrying' is a term used in boxing to refer to the act of deflecting or warding off a punch. It is a defensive maneuver that involves using one's own arm or hand to deflect or block an opponent's attack. The other options 'defending', 'dubbing', and 'dodging' do not accurately describe the action of blocking punches in boxing.

subject: English Language
explanation: In the context of boxing, a 'round' refers to a period of fighting in the ring. Boxing matches are typically divided into several rounds. The other options 'lap', 'leg', and 'turn' do not accurately describe periods of a boxing match.

subject: English Language
explanation: In boxing, a 'knockout' refers to a situation where one boxer is rendered unconscious due to a punch from the opponent. This term is used when a boxer wins a match because the opponent is unable to continue fighting. The other options 'default', 'unanimity', and 'luck' do not accurately describe the method of winning in this context.
